dependencies: Global sourcing of products is an important factor in financial performance
Suppliers must comply with applicable laws including labor, safety, anti-corruption and environmental laws
Ability to find qualified suppliers who uphold standards and access products in timely manner and large volumes
Significant challenges with suppliers located outside the U.S.
risks: Political and economic instability in countries where suppliers are located
Suppliers failure to meet terms and conditions or supplier standards
Labor problems experienced by suppliers and manufacturers
Availability of raw materials to suppliers
Extreme weather events impacting growing, manufacturing, mining and harvesting
Disruption or delay in transportation of merchandise from suppliers
U.S. and international trade policies, tariffs, trade barriers and restrictions
Trade sanctions imposed between countries and entities
Limitation on exportation or importation of certain goods
Supply chain disruption and production challenges
Labor shortages and increases in labor costs at suppliers

regulatory_risks: Changes in tax laws, regulations and interpretations
Imposition of tariffs, trade barriers, export and import restrictions
Changes in healthcare laws (including Medicare and Medicaid)
DEA and state-level regulations on controlled substances
State and federal data breach notification laws
Foreign Direct Investment rules in India
Competition and antitrust laws (U.S., Canada, Mexico, India)
Foreign ownership restrictions on eCommerce and retail operations (India)
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
Inflation Reduction Act drug pricing policies

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions between countries and entities
Increased costs of procuring products due to economic sanctions
Disruption in supply of products from sanctioned regions
Armed hostilities in Red Sea and surrounding areas affecting ocean carrier vessels to Suez Canal
Geopolitical tensions affecting operations in countries where company operates or suppliers are located
War and civil unrest in regions where goods are transported
Conflicts affecting macroeconomic conditions and consumer spending
